!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!   JEFF-4   !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!1225 1451   11
TENDL-2023 evaluation was taken as reference as a result          1225 1451   12
of analysis of neutron production (multiplicities, energy spectra 1225 1451   13
and double differential cross sections) [1].                      1225 1451   14
Residual product multiplicities in MF=6, MT=5 were replaced by    1225 1451   15
PADF-2 evaluations [2] as follows. Since PADF-2 uses  MF=10, MT=5 1225 1451   16
representation for residual product cross sections, their         1225 1451   17
multiplicities were obtained by dividing by the total nonelastic  1225 1451   18
reaction cross section MF=3, MT=5 from the reference evaluation,  1225 1451   19
and written using the reference energy grid from MF=6, MT=5.      1225 1451   20
Residual products from PADF-2 evaluation, which are not available 1225 1451   21
in reference evaluation, were added to MF=6, MT=5 using LAW=0.    1225 1451   22
There were few cases of residual products from reference file     1225 1451   23
in ground and metastable state, for which PADF-2 evaluation       1225 1451   24
considers only a single state, therefore reference multiplicities 1225 1451   25
were not altered. Same concerns photon production, which is not   1225 1451   26
provided in PADF-2.                                               1225 1451   27

Data evaluation was peformed using experimental data [1-27] and   1225 1451   45
results of model calculations.                                    1225 1451   46
                                                                  1225 1451   47
Cross sections were calculated using various codes and nuclear    1225 1451   48
models [28-32]. Results served both for comparison and control,   1225 1451   49
and were used directly for evaluation.                            1225 1451   50
                                                                  1225 1451   51
The following codes were used:                                    1225 1451   52
                                                                  1225 1451   53
 - ALICE/ASH with emission of heavy clusters [28],                1225 1451   54
                                                                  1225 1451   55
 - TALYS [29] applying different models for the calculation of    1225 1451   56
   nuclear level density with ldmodel parameter from 1 to 3,      1225 1451   57
                                                                  1225 1451   58
 - TALYS-G [30] implementing the GDH model of M.Blann,            1225 1451   59
                                                                  1225 1451   60
 - CEM03 [31],                                                    1225 1451   61
                                                                  1225 1451   62
 - PHITS [32]                                                     1225 1451   63
                                                                  1225 1451   64

the time of the evaluation.                                       1225 1451   66

TENDL-2023 (TALYS Evaluated Nuclear Data Library)                 1225 1451  233
                                                                  1225 1451  234
Mg024 proton general purpose library                              1225 1451  235
                                                                  1225 1451  236
Author: A.J. Koning, IAEA                                         1225 1451  237
                                                                  1225 1451  238
Ref.: A.J. Koning, D. Rochman, J.-Ch. Sublet, N. Dzysiuk,         1225 1451  239
      M. Fleming, and S. van der Marck, TENDL: Complete nuclear   1225 1451  240
      data library for innovative nuclear science and             1225 1451  241
      technology, Nuclear Data Sheets 155, 1 (2019).              1225 1451  242
                                                                  1225 1451  243
************** G E N E R A L   I N F O R M A T I O N *************1225 1451  244
                                                                  1225 1451  245
This evaluated data file is based on a software system built      1225 1451  246
around the nuclear model code TALYS [kon07], version 2.0. It is   1225 1451  247
part of a large collection of isotopic evaluations, all created by1225 1451  248
running the system with either adjusted or default input          1225 1451  249
parameters. This means that the data in the entire collection have1225 1451  250
been tested in detail for many isotopes against individual        1225 1451  251
experimental data, while for other isotopes it is only as good as 1225 1451  252
the global quality of TALYS at the moment. The mutual quality of  1225 1451  253
all individual data files is however consistent: The same set of  1225 1451  254
nuclear models is used and, equally important, the same ENDF-6    1225 1451  255
formatting procedures for each isotope. The data file has been    1225 1451  256
created automatically using the ENDF-6 format generator TEFAL.    1225 1451  257
                                                                  1225 1451  258
The resulting data file provides a complete representation of     1225 1451  259
nuclear data needed for transport, activation, damage, heating,   1225 1451  260
radioactivity, and shielding applications over the incident       1225 1451  261
proton energy range from 0.25 to 200. MeV.                        1225 1451  262
                                                                  1225 1451  263
All transport and activation data for neutrons, charged particles,1225 1451  264
photons and residual nuclides are filed. This includes cross      1225 1451  265
sections, angular distributions, double-differential spectra,     1225 1451  266
isomeric production, discrete and continuum photon production     1225 1451  267
cross sections, residual production cross sections and recoils.   1225 1451  268
This general purpose evaluation can thus be used as both          1225 1451  269
transport and activation library.                                 1225 1451  270
                                                                  1225 1451  271
**** N U C L I D E    S P E C I F I C   I N F O R M A T I O N ****1225 1451  272
                                                                  1225 1451  273
None                                                              1225 1451  274
                                                                  1225 1451  275
***************************** T A L Y S **************************1225 1451  276
                                                                  1225 1451  277
TALYS is a computer code system for the prediction and analysis   1225 1451  278
of nuclear reactions. TALYS simulates reactions that involve      1225 1451  279
neutrons, gamma-rays, protons, deuterons, tritons, helions and    1225 1451  280
alpha-particles, in the 1 keV - 200 MeV energy range. This is     1225 1451  281
achieved by implementing a suite of nuclear reaction models into  1225 1451  282
a single code system. This nuclear data file is based on a        1225 1451  283
theoretical analysis that utilizes the optical model, compound    1225 1451  284
nucleus statistical theory, fission, direct reactions and         1225 1451  285
pre-equilibrium processes, in combination with databases and      1225 1451  286
models for nuclear structure. The following output of TALYS is    1225 1451  287
stored in this data file:                                         1225 1451  288
                                                                  1225 1451  289
- Elastic and non-elastic cross sections                          1225 1451  290
- Elastic scattering angular distributions                        1225 1451  291
- Recoil data                                                     1225 1451  292
- Total particle cross sections, e.g. (p,xn), (p,xp),..           1225 1451  293
- Total particle energy spectra                                   1225 1451  294
- Total particle double-differential spectra                      1225 1451  295
- Isomeric and ground state cross sections                        1225 1451  296
- Residual production cross sections                              1225 1451  297
                                                                  1225 1451  298
For a description of the used nuclear models and their            1225 1451  299
parameterization we refer to [kon19]. If for this specific        1225 1451  300
nuclide we deviate from the global parameterization, it is made   1225 1451  301
explicit above under Nuclide Specific Information.                1225 1451  302
                                                                  1225 1451  303
***************** F I L E   I N F O R M A T I O N ****************1225 1451  304
                                                                  1225 1451  305
##### MF1: GENERAL INFORMATION                                    1225 1451  306
                                                                  1225 1451  307
- MT451    : Descriptive data and directory                       1225 1451  308
                                                                  1225 1451  309
This text and the full directory of used MF/MT sections.          1225 1451  310
                                                                  1225 1451  311
##### MF3: REACTION CROSS SECTIONS                                1225 1451  312
                                                                  1225 1451  313
All the data present in the following MT-sections have been       1225 1451  314
calculated with TALYS, possibly normalized to experimental data.  1225 1451  315
If the maximal cross section in an excitation function over the   1225 1451  316
whole energy range does not exceed 1.e-9 b, the MT-number is not  1225 1451  317
included. Cross sections lower than 1.e-20 b are assumed to have  1225 1451  318
no physical meaning and are set to zero. All MT numbers extend up 1225 1451  319
to the highest incident energy.                                   1225 1451  320
The following reaction channels/MT numbers are generally included:1225 1451  321
                                                                  1225 1451  322
- MT2      : Elastic scattering cross section: nuclear +          1225 1451  323
             interference terms                                   1225 1451  324
                                                                  1225 1451  325
Obtained by integrating the "nuclear-plus-interference" angular   1225 1451  326
distributions of MF=6.  Note that because of the interference     1225 1451  327
effect, the tabulations in both MF=6 and MF=3 can be negative at  1225 1451  328
some energies and angles.                                         1225 1451  329
                                                                  1225 1451  330
- MT5      : (p,anything) cross section                           1225 1451  331
                                                                  1225 1451  332
MT5 contains the reactions which can not be stored in any other   1225 1451  333
MT-number. The information of MF3/MT5 can be combined with        1225 1451  334
MF6/MT5 to obtain residual production cross sections particle     1225 1451  335
production cross sections and (double-)differential cross         1225 1451  336
sections.                                                         1225 1451  337
                                                                  1225 1451  338
##### MF6: PRODUCT ENERGY-ANGLE DISTRIBUTIONS                     1225 1451  339
                                                                  1225 1451  340
In MF6 we store all secondary energy, angle, and energy-angle     1225 1451  341
distributions, as well as all residual and discrete + continuum   1225 1451  342
photon production cross sections for channels other than capture  1225 1451  343
(MT102) and reactions to discrete levels (MT51,etc.).             1225 1451  344
All data are generated with TALYS.                                1225 1451  345
                                                                  1225 1451  346
- MT5      : (p,anything) yields and energy-angle distributions   1225 1451  347
                                                                  1225 1451  348
MT5 contains, in this order,                                      1225 1451  349
For neutrons up to alpha particles and photons:                   1225 1451  350
- the production yields per particle for the whole energy range   1225 1451  351
- the secondary energy-angle distributions per particles which are1225 1451  352
  not represented by explicit MT numbers. On a secondary energy   1225 1451  353
  grid the relative emission spectra are given together with the  1225 1451  354
  parameters for the Kalbach systematics for angular              1225 1451  355
  distributions. Inelastic scattering cross sections for discrete 1225 1451  356
  states have been broadened and added to the continuum spectra.  1225 1451  357
For each residual product:                                        1225 1451  358
- the production yields for the whole energy range.               1225 1451  359
- the recoil                                                      1225 1451  360
These distributions can be multiplied with MF3/MT5 to get         1225 1451  361
the production cross sections and (double-)differential cross     1225 1451  362
sections.                                                         1225 1451  363
                                                                  1225 1451  364
##### MF8: RADIOACTIVE DECAY DATA                                 1225 1451  365
                                                                  1225 1451  366
For reactions to isomers, MF8 designates where the information    1225 1451  367
for isomeric versus ground state production, or residual          1225 1451  368
production can be found, i.e. for each MT number it points to     1225 1451  369
either MF6, MF9 or MF10.                                          1225 1451  370
                                                                  1225 1451  371
##### MF33: COVARIANCES OF NEUTRON CROSS SECTIONS                 1225 1451  372
                                                                  1225 1451  373
In MF33, we store the covariances of most, but not all, reaction  1225 1451  374
channels that are given in MF3. All covariances are generated     1225 1451  375
with the TASMAN code [kon19], which generates TALYS runs using    1225 1451  376
a Monte Carlo parameter method.                                   1225 1451  377
For each MT-number, the information consists of:                  1225 1451  378
1. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTa, Ea (variances)                1225 1451  379
2. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTa, Eb (correlations for the      1225 1451  380
   same reaction channel between different energies)              1225 1451  381
3. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTb, Ea (correlations between      1225 1451  382
   different reaction channels for the same energy)               1225 1451  383
4. Covariances for MTa, Ea and MTb, Eb (correlations between      1225 1451  384
   different reaction channels for different energies)            1225 1451  385
Classes 1 and 2 are covered by NI-type subsubsections with        1225 1451  386
flag LB=5, followed by a LB=8 section which contains              1225 1451  387
the cross section variances multiplied by 1.e-3. Next, classes 3  1225 1451  388
and 4 are covered by flag LB=6 to account for the different       1225 1451  389
energy grids that two correlating MT-sections may have. Since     1225 1451  390
the total correlation matrix is fully symmetric, we only need to  1225 1451  391
include the correlation elements between different MT-numbers     1225 1451  392
once, and we do that in the subsection with the lowest of the     1225 1451  393
two MT-numbers. We include covariance information for all the     1225 1451  394
total cross sections, the lumped partial cross sections and the   1225 1451  395
individual inelastic cross sections per level (MT51,...). We do   1225 1451  396
not include covariances for MT600 and higher MT-numbers. For the  1225 1451  397
most important channels, we include cross-channel correlations.   1225 1451  398
                                                                  1225 1451  399
